---
title: "Fish-PACE User Group"
favicon: "favicon.ico"
image: "images/pace-nmfs.png"
links:
  - label: Slack
    url: "https://fish-pace.slack.com/"
  - label: GitHub
    url: "https://github.com/fish-pace"
  - label: JupyterHub
    url: "https://cryointhecloud.com/"
  - label: Events
    url: https://pace.oceansciences.org/events.htm
  - label: Email
    url: "mailto:eli.holmes@noaa.gov"
output:
  postcards::trestles
site: distill::distill_website
---

The Fish-PACE User Group serves as a forum for connecting, supporting, and growing the fisheries and ocean ecosystem user community around NASA’s PACE mission and new ocean color products, with the goal to advance applications for fisheries, marine ecosystems, and coastal decision-making. We support a Slack channel, events, trainings (workshops and hackweeks) and a community JupyterHub. Join our Slack and watch the PACE Applications [calendar](https://pace.oceansciences.org/events.htm) for announcements.

## Upcoming Events

* June 26 12PM EDT - Fish-PACE User Group Onboarding: Intro to earthaccess Python package and on-boarding to the [CryoCloud JupyterHub](https://cryointhecloud.com/) for Fish-Pace UG members. This is a repeat of the 19th material above; no need to come to both. [Register](https://zoom.us/meeting/register/wmtzgrJxSk-wQFT7vpNCDA). 
* [CoastWatch Satellite 201 Summer Lecture Series](https://coastwatch-training.github.io/trainings/upcoming/sat201_26.html) July 10, 17, 24 and 31, 2026, Virtual and free to all, Fridays 11am - 1pm (Pacific Time). July 24th session: Ryan Vandermeulen presents on PACE products beyond Chlorophyll for fisheries and ocean ecosystems applications. [Register](https://forms.gle/XGtZWLqMAueXZQZX9)
* [OceanHackWeek 2026](https://oceanhackweek.org/ohw26/) will be August 24-28, 2026 at the Bamfield Marine Sciences Centre in BC, Canada. Applications closed. As in the past 2 years, Eli Holmes (NOAA) will mentor a Fish-PACE focused project.
* Fish-PACE User Group Fall Telcom. TBD but plan is Oct or Nov and virtual. We will have a series of short and lightning talks from the user community. Sign-ups will be shared on the [PACE Communinty mailing list](https://pace.oceansciences.org/pace_listserv.htm) and Fish-PACE Slack.

## Past Events

* June 19 3PM EDT - Fish-PACE User Group Onboarding: Intro to earthaccess Python package and on-boarding to the [CryoCloud JupyterHub](https://cryointhecloud.com/) for Fish-Pace UG members. Material will be repeated on the 26th. 
* [Fish-PACE HackWeek Jan 2026](https://fish-pace.github.io/hackweek-2025/) Virtual Jan 26-30, 2026
* [EDMW 2025](https://nmfs-opensci.github.io/EDMW-EarthData-Workshop-2025/) Introduction to using earth data in the cloud for scientific workflows: PACE hyperspectral ocean color data
* [HyperDawg](https://github.com/ocean-satellite-tools/hyperdawg): Bi-weekly informal discussions and experimentation
* [NOAA Fisheries Friday HackHours](https://nmfs-opensci.github.io/NMFSHackDays-2026/): Though not hyperspectral focused, much of the training is relevant to working with hyperspectral data.

## Partners

[NOAA Fisheries](https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/about/office-science-and-technology), [PACE Applications](https://pace.oceansciences.org/applications.htm), [CoastWatch](https://coastwatch.noaa.gov/cwn/index.html), [CryoCloud](https://cryointhecloud.com/).
